what_it_does: This file is the root stylesheet for the ConceptV Design System; it contains only a header comment and a single @import that pulls in colors_and_type.css, presenting itself as the one file consumers should link.
observations: The entire stylesheet consists of a detailed block comment plus one @import rule.
It claims to be the single entry point for tokens, type presets, and the surface-zone registry.
It explicitly states that app-specific and kit-specific chrome styles are intentionally not imported here.
No CSS rules, selectors, variables, or media queries are defined in this file.
standouts: The header frames the file as the canonical public entry point for the design system.
Only colors_and_type.css is imported, despite the comment mentioning multiple sources.
It deliberately excludes app/app.css and ui_kits/*.css from the shared load path.
conventions: Uses a verbose, structured block comment to document intent and usage.
Uses @import to compose the design system from separate sheets.
Separates core design-system tokens from app/kit implementation styles.
concerns: The stated scope (tokens, type presets, surface-zone registry) is broader than the single @import actually present.
There is no visible fallback or error handling if colors_and_type.css fails to load.
No versioning or cache-busting hints are present on the import URL.
notes: A thin, self-documented root CSS entry point that delegates core styling to colors_and_type.css and leaves app/kit chrome for other surfaces to load.
questions: Where are the type presets and surface-zone registry sheets, since only colors_and_type.css is imported?
Do consumers load app/app.css and ui_kits/*.css manually, and in what order?
Is colors_and_type.css intended to contain all tokens, type, and registry content, or are additional imports missing?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to serve as a single entry point that pulls in tokens, type presets, and the surface-zone registry; the actual content only imports colors_and_type.css, so it partially matches the described scope.
apparent_themes: Separation of core design system from app/kit chrome
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to create a stable, minimal, and well-documented root entry point for consumers, exposing only the core design-system foundations while letting each surface own its own chrome styles.
novelty: The explicit, documented exclusion of app/kit chrome from the shared root entry point is a deliberate architectural signal, though the pattern itself is common.
contribution: This file contributes a documented root CSS entry point that centralizes the core token and type import and keeps implementation-specific chrome out of the shared design-system load path, making the boundary between core system and app/kit surfaces explicit.
summary_for_embedding: A minimal root CSS file for the ConceptV Design System that imports colors_and_type.css and documents itself as the canonical consumer entry point while excluding app and kit chrome.
intention_for_embedding: This file was meant to provide a single, stable link target for consumers that loads the core design-system tokens and typography while leaving surface-specific styles to their own loaders.
